content: The Global Interpreter Lock (GIL) is a mutex in CPython that guarantees only one native thread can execute Python bytecode at any moment, protecting reference‑counted objects and other interpreter state from concurrent modification [1]. It was added because CPython’s reference‑counting memory management would be unsafe without a single lock, offering a simple and fast solution for single‑threaded
